title: townsendia scapigera
intro: Townsendia scapigera, also known as Townsend's rosinweed, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family that grows in the western United States and Canada. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant's roots and leaves have been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Townsendia scapigera can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and salves, making it a versatile remedy in traditional herbal medicine.
